question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to the metrics endpoint that causes exporter.collect() to reject with an unhandled promise rejection (no .catch() handler on the Promise chain at line 161), crashing the Node.js process via the unhandledRejection event and shutting down all transaction confirmation endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of requests to the metrics endpoint (if authentication is disabled) causing exporter.collect() to be called concurrently many times, and if the PrometheusExporter does not handle concurrent collect() calls safely, causing a race condition that corrupts internal metric state, leading to a crash or incorrect metric data that masks real service degradation?

'') + req.route?.path in toOpenApiPath() to produce a label string of tens of thousands of characters, which when stored across requestTotalCounter, durationHistogram, requestSizeHistogram, and responseSizeHistogram, consumes disproportionate memory per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes Date.now() - startTime to produce a negative duration value (e.g., due to system clock adjustment via NTP between request start and finish), causing durationHistogram.record() to record a negative value that the OpenTelemetry SDK rejects with an exception, propagating through the finish event listener and crashing the process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes next() to never be called (e.g., if an exception is thrown before next() at line 213), combined with the fact that inFlightCounter.add(1) has already been called at line 169, permanently leaking the in-flight count and causing the request to hang without a response, consuming a connection slot indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the URL parsing at new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') succeeds but produces a pathname of '/' or an empty string, causing normalizedPath to be '/' which never equals metricsPath, but the subsequent toOpenApiPath() call in the finish handler returns a path starting with apiPrefix + '//' (double slash), creating a unique label that pollutes the metrics registry?
